```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = [] # Added for '>'
                    corridors = [] # Added for ':'
                    monsters_rat = [] # Added for 'r'
                    monsters_adjacent_to_agent = [] # New: to track monsters within striking distance

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': # Explicitly identify '>' as stairs down
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': # Explicitly identify ':' as corridors
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': # Explicitly identify 'r' as rat monster
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if monster is adjacent to agent
                                if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
                                    if abs(abs_x - agent_x) <= 1 and abs(abs_y - agent_y) <= 1:
                                        monsters_adjacent_to_agent.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, functional-impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  # Report stairs down
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    # Report corridors
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: # Report rat monsters (assuming 'r' is a rat for now)
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_adjacent_to_agent: # New: Report monsters within striking distance
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_adjacent_to_agent)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] # Corrected variable name
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, functional-impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, functional-impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, functional-impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
                    if walls:
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, can be moved into but is lethal) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        # Changed perception output here to avoid "functional-impassable" which caused confusion for Q5.
                        # Now it directly states it's lethal upon entry but does not physically block.
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal upon entry, does not physically block movement) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```

```python
    )

        extracted_info.append(

        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3] if marker != -1]
            
            if end_markers:
                map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    stairs_down = []
                    corridors = []
                    monsters_rat = []
                    monsters_within_striking_distance = []

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '>': 
                                stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == ':': 
                                corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'r': 
                                monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                # Check if rat is within striking distance (adjacent, including diagonally)
                                if (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (agent_x != abs_x or agent_y != abs_y)):
                                    monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, functional-impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if stairs_down:  
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    if corridors:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                    if monsters_rat: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters (rat) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Monsters within striking distance of agent: {sorted(list(set(monsters_within_striking_distance)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n)|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): 
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if ((len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message)) and
           (not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```
